source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md → user-blocking-plan-mode-approval (full arc: plan emitted, then user approves)
notes: Full plan-mode approval-gate arc on one live codex rollout session. The driver enters Plan Mode with the `/plan` slash command (a TUI collaboration mode — codex has no headless plan-mode entry), then prompts for an immediate proposed plan in an empty fresh cwd. Turn 1: codex emits an assistant `final_answer` wrapping a complete `<proposed_plan>…</proposed_plan>` block, then `task_complete`. The codex parser (parser.go parseCodexMessage) synthesizes a SYNTHETIC tool_use ExitPlanMode (id=codex-proposed-plan) from that span; the open tool call survives task_complete (turn_done), so the classifier routes working→waiting instead of working→ready — the plan-review gate. This is the same waiting branch claudecode reaches via a real ExitPlanMode tool_use (scenario-meanings names the Codex <proposed_plan> block as the plan_mode anchor). The user then APPROVES by sending a follow-up that exits the gate: the user message clears the open tool name (ClearToolNames) and the session returns to working. The approval prompt directs codex to EXECUTE a multi-step task (create a.txt/b.txt/c.txt, run `ls`, then summarize) so the recovery turn spans several wall-clock seconds with distinct tool calls. This is deliberate: the daemon coalesces transcript activity per session in a 2s debounce window, and a turn-end short-circuits it — a trivial single-reply recovery (the earlier 'confirm in one sentence' prompt) landed user-message + agent-reply in one sub-2s burst, so the first activity event already saw a completed turn and the classifier jumped waiting→ready, debounce-coalescing the brief working blip. With real intermediate work the first post-approval activity observes an in-progress turn → working, and task_complete arrives seconds later → ready, making the waiting→working→ready recovery OBSERVABLE. Observable arc: ready→working→waiting→working→ready, all on the SAME session_id — the waiting episode is visible (no skip-over) and the cell ends in ready. Observable ONLY via the interactive TUI: headless `codex exec` cannot enter plan mode and is single-shot. The `/plan` slash toggles mode without producing a turn (no task_complete), so the driver uses a trailing sleep after the slash rather than wait_turn. The daemon sees the proc-PID pre-session born ready, the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then working, waiting, working again, ready, and process_exited at REPL teardown.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
Plan-request prompt (in Plan Mode) → working on the rollout session
Agent emits a <proposed_plan> block → synthetic ExitPlanMode keeps a tool call open across task_complete → turn ends in waiting, not ready (the plan-review gate)
The waiting episode is visible and persists while the agent is gated on the user's plan decision — no skip-over to ready before the user approves

User approves and asks the agent to implement the plan → session returns to working (waiting→working) on the same session; the recovery turn does real multi-step work (create files, run ls) so this working span is observable and not coalesced
Agent finishes executing the approved task (files created, ls run, summary given) → ready (working→ready)
Driver tears down the REPL after the recovery — process_exited on the rollout session (single live session across both turns)
